title: The proof of the $l^2$ Decoupling Conjecture

thm:1.1.summary: The $l^2$ decoupling theorem for a compact $C^2$ hypersurface $S\subset\mathbb{R}^n$ with positive definite second fundamental form: if $\widehat f$ is supported in the $\delta$-neighborhood $\mathcal{N}_\delta$ of $S$, then $\|f\|_p \lesssim_\epsilon \delta^{-\frac{n-1}{4}+\frac{n+1}{2p}-\epsilon}(\sum_{\theta\in\mathcal{P}_\delta}\|f_\theta\|_p^2)^{1/2}$ for $p\ge\frac{2(n+1)}{n-1}$. The sharp $l^2$ estimate; resolves the decoupling conjecture for paraboloid/sphere.

thm:1.2.summary: Companion $l^2$ decoupling for the truncated cone $C^{n-1}$: $\|f\|_p \lesssim_\epsilon \delta^{-\frac{n-2}{4}+\frac{n}{2p}-\epsilon}(\sum_\theta\|f_\theta\|_p^2)^{1/2}$ for $p\ge\frac{2n}{n-2}$, and $\delta^{-\epsilon}$ loss only for $2\le p\le\frac{2n}{n-2}$. Proved in Section 8 by reducing the cone to the paraboloid one dimension down.

thm:2.1.summary: The Tomas–Stein restriction estimate: for a compact $C^2$ hypersurface with nonzero Gaussian curvature, $\|\widehat{fd\sigma}\|_{L^p(\mathbb{R}^n)}\lesssim\|f\|_{L^2(S)}$ for $p\ge\frac{2(n+1)}{n-1}$. Classical input recalled for context; decoupling reproves and refines its discrete analogues.

thm:2.2.summary: Exponential-sum form of discrete restriction: for a $\delta^{1/2}$-separated set $\Lambda\subset S$ and $R\gtrsim\delta^{-1}$, $(\tfrac{1}{|B_R|}\int_{B_R}|\sum_{\xi\in\Lambda}a_\xi e(\xi\cdot x)|^p)^{1/p}\lesssim_\epsilon \delta^{\frac{n+1}{2p}-\frac{n-1}{4}-\epsilon}\|a_\xi\|_2$. Immediate consequence of $l^2$ decoupling.

thm:2.3.summary: Discrete restriction for lattice points on the paraboloid $P^{n-1}(N)$: sharp $L^p(\mathbb{T}^n)$ bounds for the exponential sum $\sum_{\xi\in P^{n-1}(N)}a_\xi e(\xi\cdot x)$, including the conjectured exponent for $p\ge\frac{2(n+2)}{n-1}$ when $n\ge4$. Follows from $l^2$ decoupling plus a lattice-point count.

thm:2.4.summary: Optimal Strichartz estimates for the periodic Schrödinger evolution on rational and irrational tori: $\|e^{it\Delta}\phi\|_{L^p(\mathbb{T}^{n-1}\times I)}\lesssim_\epsilon N^{\frac{n-1}{2}-\frac{n+1}{p}+\epsilon}|I|^{1/p}\|\phi\|_2$ for $p\ge\frac{2(n+1)}{n-1}$, with constant independent of the torus parameters. Resolves conjectures of Bourgain on irrational tori.

thm:2.7.summary: Discrete restriction for lattice points on the sphere (eq. 11) holds for $p\ge\frac{2(n-1)}{n-3}$ when $n\ge4$ — progress toward Conjecture 2.6 — obtained by applying $l^2$ decoupling for the sphere.

thm:2.8.summary: $L^4$ discrete restriction for an arbitrary finite point set $\Lambda\subset P^2$: $(\tfrac1{|B_R|}\int_{B_R}|\sum_{\xi\in\Lambda}a_\xi e(\xi\cdot x)|^4)^{1/4}\lesssim_\epsilon|\Lambda|^\epsilon\|a_\xi\|_2$ for $R$ large. Equivalent to a near-optimal additive-energy bound; ties decoupling to incidence geometry.

thm:2.9.summary: Szemerédi–Trotter: $O(|\mathcal{L}|+|\mathcal{P}|+(|\mathcal{L}||\mathcal{P}|)^{2/3})$ incidences between lines and points in the plane. Cited as the incidence-geometry benchmark that decoupling-derived energy bounds recover or refine.

thm:2.11.summary: Pach–Sharir bound $O(N^2\log N)$ on repetitions of a fixed angle among $N$ planar points. Context: decoupling supplies an alternative route to such counting estimates.

conjecture:2.6.summary: Conjectured sharp discrete restriction for lattice points $\mathcal{F}_{n,N^2}$ on the sphere of radius $N$: $\|\sum a_\xi e(\xi\cdot x)\|_{L^p(\mathbb{T}^n)}\lesssim_\epsilon N^{\frac{n-2}{2}-\frac{n}{p}+\epsilon}\|a_\xi\|_2$ for $p\ge\frac{2n}{n-2}$. Target that thm:2.7 partially attains.

conjecture:2.10.summary: The Erdős unit-distance conjecture: $N$ points in the plane span $\lesssim_\epsilon N^{1+\epsilon}$ unit distances. Mentioned to delimit what decoupling-based energy estimates can and cannot reach.

prop:2.15.summary: Cubic additive energy bound: for $S=P^1$ or $S^1$ and any $\Lambda\subset S$, $\mathbb{E}_3(\Lambda)\lesssim_\epsilon|\Lambda|^{7/2+\epsilon}$. Sharp; derived from the $L^6$ ($n=2$) case of decoupling.

thm:2.16.summary: Decoupling for arbitrary point sets distributed over the caps of $\mathcal{P}_\delta$ on a curved hypersurface, at the critical exponent $p=\frac{2(n+1)}{n-1}$. The bridge result powering the additive-energy and incidence applications (thm:2.8, prop:2.15, thm:2.17).

thm:2.17.summary: Cubic additive energy of an annular lattice set $A_R'$: $\mathbb{E}_3(A_R')\lesssim_\epsilon|A_R'|^{3+\epsilon}$. Used to count solutions of the Diophantine inequalities in thm:2.18–2.19.

thm:2.18.summary: For fixed $k\ge2$ the system $|n_1^k+n_2^k+n_3^k-n_4^k-n_5^k-n_6^k|\le CN^{k-2}$, $n_1+n_2+n_3=n_4+n_5+n_6$ has $O(N^{3+\epsilon})$ solutions with $n_i\sim N$. A Vinogradov-mean-value-flavored count obtained from decoupling for the curve.

thm:2.19.summary: Mixed-degree $L^6$ exponential-sum bound $\int_{|x|\le1}\int_0^\lambda|\sum_{n\sim N}e(xn^2+yn^k)|^6\,dx\,dy\lesssim_\epsilon\lambda N^{3+\epsilon}+N^{4-k+\epsilon}$ for $k\ge4$, giving $O(N^{3+\epsilon})$ solutions of a coupled square/$k$-th-power Diophantine inequality. Consequence of $l^2$ decoupling for the moment-curve-type surface.

def:3.1.summary: An $N$-tube: an $N^{1/2}\times\cdots\times N^{1/2}\times N$ rectangular box dual to a cap $\theta\in\mathcal{P}_\delta$; a family of $N$-tubes is separated if $\lesssim C$ tubes share any orientation. The geometric atom of the wave-packet calculus.

def:3.2.summary: An $N$-function $f=\sum_{T\in\mathcal{T}(f)}f_T$ built from separated $N$-tubes with $|f_T|\le\phi_T$, $\|f_T\|_p\sim|T|^{1/p}$, and $\widehat{f_T}\subset\theta(T)$; it is balanced if the tube counts per direction are comparable. Reduces general $\mathcal{N}_\delta$-functions to a combinatorially clean model.

lem:3.3.summary: Norm formula for $N$-functions: $\|f\|_{p,1/N}\sim N^{\frac{n+1}{2p}}(\sum_\theta|\mathcal{T}(f,\theta)|^{2/p})^{1/2}$, and for balanced $f$, $\sim N^{\frac{n+1}{2p}}M(f)^{\frac12-\frac1p}|\mathcal{T}(f)|^{1/p}$ with $M(f)$ the number of active directions. Converts $L^p$ norms into tube counts.

lem:3.4.summary: (i) Every $N$-function decomposes into $O(\log|\mathcal{T}(f)|)$ balanced $N$-functions; (ii) for balanced $f$, $\|f\|_{p,1/N}\sim\|f\|_{2,1/N}^{2/p}\|f\|_{\infty,1/N}^{1-2/p}$ (interpolation reversibility). Lets the argument assume balanced functions at logarithmic cost.

lem:3.5.summary: Wave-packet decomposition: any $f$ Fourier-supported in $\mathcal{N}_\delta$ splits as $f=\sum_{\lambda}\lambda f_\lambda$ over dyadic heights with each $f_\lambda$ a $(N=\delta^{-1})$-function satisfying $\lambda^pN^{\frac{n+1}{2}}|\mathcal{T}(f_\lambda)|\le\|\lambda f_\lambda\|_{p,\delta}^p\lesssim\|f\|_{p,\delta}^p$. The reduction from arbitrary functions to $N$-functions that the whole proof runs on.

prop:4.1.summary: Parabolic rescaling: if the decoupling constant $K_p(\delta/\sigma)$ holds at scale $\delta/\sigma$, then for any $\sigma$-cap $\tau$, $\|f_\tau\|_p\lesssim K_p(\delta/\sigma)(\sum_{\theta\subset\tau}\|f_\theta\|_p^2)^{1/2}$. The affine-invariance step enabling the multiscale/self-improvement iteration.

def:5.1.summary: Caps $\tau_1,\dots,\tau_n$ on $P^{n-1}$ are $\nu$-transverse if the parallelepiped spanned by unit normals $v_i$ at the $\tau_i$ has volume $>\nu$. The transversality hypothesis under which the multilinear estimate holds.

thm:5.3.summary:
Linear-versus-multilinear reduction: assuming lower-dimensional decoupling ($K_{p,d}(\delta')\lesssim_\epsilon\delta'^{-\epsilon}$ for $2\le d\le n-1$), the linear decoupling constant satisfies $K_{p,n}(\delta)\le C_
u\delta^{-\epsilon(
u)}C_{p,n}(\delta,
u)$ with $\epsilon(
u)	o0$ as $
u	o0$. Reduces the theorem to the multilinear (transverse) estimate — the hinge of the proof.

prop:5.5.summary: Bourgain–Guth-type two-scale inequality for $\widehat{gd\sigma}$ on $P^2$: bounds the global norm by $K^\epsilon$ times $l^2$-sums over $1/K$- and $1/K^{1/2}$-caps plus a $K^{10}C_{p,3}$ multilinear remainder over $R^{-1/2}$-caps. The broad/narrow dichotomy made quantitative.

prop:5.6.summary: Cap-localized version of prop:5.5: the same broad/narrow split applied inside a single $\delta$-cap $\tau$, valid for $R>\delta^{-2}$. The rescaled building block iterated in the induction-on-scales.

thm:6.1.summary: Multilinear restriction/Kakeya estimate: for $\nu$-transverse caps $\tau_1,\dots,\tau_n$ on $P^{n-1}$ with $\widehat{f_i}$ supported in the $\delta$-neighborhood of $\tau_i$, $\|(\prod_i|f_i|)^{1/n}\|_{L^{2n/(n-1)}(B_N)}\lesssim_{\epsilon,\nu}N^{-1/2+\epsilon}(\prod_i\|f_i\|_{L^2})^{1/n}$. The external geometric input (Bennett–Carbery–Tao / Guth multilinear Kakeya) the multilinear decoupling is built on.

prop:6.2.summary: Multilinear decoupling for $\nu$-transverse caps: $\|(\prod_i|f_i|)^{1/n}\|_{L^p(B_N)}\lesssim_{\epsilon,\nu}N^{\frac{n-1}{4}-\frac{n^2+n}{2p(n-1)}+\epsilon}(\prod_i\|f_i\|_{p(n-1)/n,\delta})^{1/n}$ for $p\ge\frac{2n}{n-1}$ (and an $l^2$-square-function variant). Multilinear restriction upgraded to a decoupling statement.

prop:6.3.summary: Self-improvement / induction-on-scales engine: inequality (38) holds with exponent $\beta=1$ and $A_1(N)=N^{\frac{n-1}{4}-\frac{n^2+n}{2p(n-1)}}$, and if it holds for $\beta\in(0,1]$ it holds for $\beta\xi$ with an explicitly improved $A_{\beta\xi}(N)=A_\beta(N^{1/2})N^{\beta\eta+\frac\gamma2(1-\beta\xi)}$. Iterating drives the exponent to the sharp value — the technical heart of the paper.

prop:8.1.summary:
Anisotropic ($L^6$, $n=2$) sector decoupling: for a sector $S$ of length $N^{-
u}$ and aperture $N^{-\mu}$ with $2\mu+
u\le1$, $2\mu\ge
u$, $\|E_Sg\|_{L^6(w_{B_N})}\lesssim_\epsilon N^{
u\epsilon}(\sum_{S'\subset S}\|E_{S'}g\|_{L^6}^2)^{1/2}$ over a finer tiling. Cone-to-paraboloid reduction step.

prop:8.2.summary: Endpoint case of prop:8.1 with aperture $N^{-\nu/2}$, decoupling a sector into aperture-$N^{-1/2}$ pieces with only $N^\epsilon$ loss. Combined with prop:8.1 it yields the cone decoupling thm:1.2.

edges.note: The linear decoupling estimate is reduced, via the linear-vs-multilinear bootstrap, to the transverse multilinear estimate.
The reduction runs through the quantitative broad/narrow ($n=3$) inequality.
Iterates the cap-localized broad/narrow inequality across scales.
Parabolic rescaling supplies the scale-invariance the iteration needs.
The 'narrow' (transverse) term is controlled by multilinear decoupling.
Rescaling a $\delta$-cap to unit scale.
Multilinear decoupling is bootstrapped from the multilinear restriction/Kakeya inequality.
The sharp exponent in prop:6.2 comes from running the prop:6.3 self-improvement iteration.
Base case of the induction uses the multilinear Kakeya bound.
Induction on scales is run on $N$-functions produced by the wave-packet decomposition.
Each induction step rescales sub-caps to the original scale.
Output of the decomposition is a sum of $N$-functions.
Balanced-function reduction is applied to the pieces.
Norm of each piece is read off via the tube-count formula.
Computes the $L^p$ norm of an $N$-function in terms of its tube data.
Decomposes an arbitrary $N$-function into balanced ones and gives reverse interpolation.
An $N$-function is assembled from separated $N$-tubes.
States the estimate under the $\nu$-transversality hypothesis.
Multilinear Kakeya holds for $\nu$-transverse caps.
Transversality is the regime in which the multilinear term is gainful.
Exponential-sum discrete restriction follows directly from $l^2$ decoupling.
Lattice-point discrete restriction on the paraboloid via decoupling plus a counting bound.
Strichartz estimates on (ir)rational tori are decoupling for the paraboloid in disguise.
Sphere decoupling gives the partial discrete-restriction bound toward Conjecture 2.6.
Establishes the conjectured estimate in a restricted exponent range.
Decoupling over points distributed in caps is extracted from the main decoupling theorem.
$L^4$ point-set restriction on $P^2$ is the $n=3$, $p=4$ instance of cap decoupling.
Cubic additive energy for $P^1$/$S^1$ from the $L^6$, $n=2$ case.
Annular-set cubic energy via cap decoupling.
Compared/combined with Szemerédi–Trotter incidence counting.
Solution count for the degree-$k$ Diophantine inequality from the annular additive-energy bound.
Mixed-degree exponential-sum bound from decoupling for the moment-curve-type surface.
Cone decoupling is assembled from the anisotropic sector-decoupling step.
And its endpoint aperture case.
Sector decoupling for the cone reduces to paraboloid decoupling one dimension lower.
Same reduction at the endpoint scale.
Tomas–Stein restriction is the continuous prototype that decoupling sharpens at the discrete level.

main_theorems.description: The $l^2$ decoupling estimates for curved hypersurfaces and the cone.

wave_packet_calculus.description: Discretize $\mathcal{N}_\delta$-functions into $N$-tubes and $N$-functions; reduce to balanced functions; record norms as tube counts.

rescaling_and_broad_narrow.description: Parabolic rescaling plus the Bourgain–Guth broad/narrow dichotomy that splits a linear estimate into a transverse multilinear piece and lower-dimensional pieces.

multilinear_core.description: Multilinear Kakeya/restriction input upgraded to multilinear decoupling, then driven to the sharp exponent by induction on scales.

cone_extension.description: Reduce decoupling for the cone to the paraboloid via anisotropic sector decoupling.

applications.description: Discrete restriction, Strichartz estimates, lattice points on the sphere, additive energy / incidence geometry, and Diophantine-inequality counts — all corollaries of decoupling.

BCT.title: On the multilinear restriction and Kakeya conjectures

BG.title: Bounds on oscillatory integral operators based on multilinear estimates

Guth.title: A short proof of the multilinear Kakeya inequality

Wol.title: Local smoothing type estimates on $L^p$ for large $p$

PS.title: Repeated angles in the plane and related problems

Bo3.title: Moment inequalities for trigonometric polynomials with spectrum in curved hypersurfaces

GOW.title: Strichartz estimates for the Schrödinger equation on irrational tori

Dem23.title: Incidence theory and discrete restriction estimates
